title: Support Diagnostics Tab Console
concept: A responsive tabbed operations workspace that prioritizes selected-site status, logs, database details, transfer history, and realistic warnings while keeping the live WordPress shell protected and usable.
focus: saved, local, and temporary state differences
flows: Select a Blueprint from a representative gallery subset, inspect JSON, validate it, confirm replacement, run progress, and update the active preview state
Change runtime settings, show unsaved reset warnings or stored Save & Reload behavior, run progress, and update the final runtime badge
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with distinct consequences, progress, saved row creation, and shell identity updates
Rename and delete saved Playgrounds with confirmation, list mutation, and active-shell fallback
Use Site Manager tabs for settings, files, Blueprint JSON, database tools, logs, and portability actions while the WordPress shell remains visible
featureCoverage: GitHub import with account connection and token warning
Blueprint gallery categories and search with representative subset label
Save temporary Playground in browser storage
Delete saved Playground with confirmation and fallback
WordPress, PHP, language, older versions, network, and multisite settings
Destructive unsaved reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
File browser with new file, new folder, upload, browse, selected editor, dirty state, and save result
Blueprint copy, download, run, validation, editor, and result state
Database driver, SQLite path, size, database download, Adminer, and phpMyAdmin
Playground, WordPress, and PHP logs with realistic warning state
Database download and Blueprint bundle portability
